revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Idera Kofun]][[ja:井寺古墳]][[zh:井寺古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name =Idera Kofun | native_name =井寺古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Kumamoto Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Idera Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|32.753103|N|130.777881|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kashima, Kumamoto|en|Kashima, Kumamoto|qid=Q1184801|lt=Kashima, Kumamoto|1=Kashima, Kumamoto}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|45|11.17|N|130|46|40.37|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Idera Kofun'''|井寺古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]] in the Idera-chō neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Kashima, Kumamoto|en|Kashima, Kumamoto|qid=Q1184801|lt=Kashima, Kumamoto|1=Kashima, Kumamoto}}, Japan. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1921, with the area under protection expanded in 2017.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/173385|title=井寺古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is believed to have been built in the middleof the 6th century, towards the end of the Kofun period.
==Overview== The Idera Kofun is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} style Kofun built on a hill southeast of Kumamoto city. On the north side of the tomb is Ukishima Kumanoimasu Shrine (Ukishima Jinja), known for its spring water. In 1857, the tumulus collapsed due to earthwork, and a stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]. It was [[:en:tomb robbery|robbed]] in 1901 to 1902. A preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] in 1916 to 1917 confirmed that it was a [[decorated kofun]]; however a comprehensive investigation was not performed until 1982 and again from 2017 to 2019, after the [[:en:2016 Kumamoto earthquake|2016 Kumamoto earthquake]]. The tumulus has been largely leveled, and no ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found. The urial facility is a single-chamber horizontal-entry stone room made of blocks of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} from {{ill|Mount Aso|en|Mount Aso|qid=Q733710|lt=Mount Aso|1=Mount Aso}}. It has an opening in the south-southwest direction. The interior, including the entrance gate, entrance passage, and stone barrier are decorated with a decorative pattern called "straight arc pattern" consisting of geometric straight lines and arcs. In addition, depending on location, "circular pattern," "ladder pattern," and "key pattern," decorations can be found. This straight arc pattern is painted in four colors: red, white, blue, and green. As the tomb had been robbed, the presence of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} is uncertain. However, [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]] records state that a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}} and a straight sword were found in the first area near the back wall, and human bones were found in the two areas on the left and right. However, the whereabouts of these items are now unknown. The Kumamoto City Museum has four straight iron swords, which are said to have come from the Idera Kofun. Based on decoration and artifacts, It is estimated that it was built around the late 5th century to early 6th century during the middleto late Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is about 11.5 kilometers from {{ill|Nishi-Kumamoto Station|en|Nishi-Kumamoto Station|qid=Q20762689|lt=Nishi-Kumamoto Station|1=Nishi-Kumamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|qid=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Kagoshima Main Line|en|Kagoshima Main Line|qid=Q1187520|lt=Kagoshima Main Line|1=Kagoshima Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|qid=Q6573273|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Kumamoto)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.town.kumamoto-kashima.lg.jp/q/aview/262/511.html Kashima City home page]{{in lang|ja}}
